exits.label: ◂ surface from the memory — back to the Reed-Walk

npcs.name: Coll
npcs.title: caught in the worst night, looping
npcs.hail: *(a soul stands at the table doing the same small action over and over — reaching for a cup, knocking it, watching it fall, flinching, and then it is whole on the table again and the reaching begins anew — trapped in a single moment it relives without end, and it does not see you, because in the memory you are not there; you have stepped, with your dead-touched senses, into the locked room of someone else's worst night)*

npcs.name: the Frozen Woman
npcs.title: the other one in the memory
npcs.hail: *(a second figure stands frozen mid-gesture by the chair — a woman caught at the exact instant the memory keeps returning to, her face fixed in an expression the dreamer reads as fury and that you, looking closer with the cold, are not so sure about; she is not looping, only held, a still photograph the dreamer's guilt has painted over and over until it no longer quite matches whatever truly stood there)*

npcs.name: the Way Out
npcs.title: the door the dreamer won't see
npcs.hail: *(there is a window at the room's far wall, and through it grey morning light — a way out of the locked night, an after to the worst moment, the simple fact that the night ended and a life continued past it — and the dreamer never once looks at it, because a soul reliving its worst moment has decided, somewhere, that the moment is where it deserves to stay, and so the door that was always open goes forever unseen)*

variants.text: *(For the first time the looping falters — the cup half-reached, forgotten — as something about you reaches it through the memory's walls.)* …who — *(it blinks, surfacing)* — who's *there?* You're not — you're not *part* of it. *(it looks around the locked room, confused)* The *night* doesn't *have* a *cold* *stranger* in it. The *night* has *me*, and *her*, and the *cup*, and the — *(it shudders)* — the *thing* I *said.* *(it focuses on you, and there's a terrible exhausted hope in it)* You've — you've been *outside.* I can *tell.* You *know* something. About — *(barely)* — about what *really* *happened* that *night. Tell* me, cold one. I've *relived* it *ten* *thousand* *times* and I *still* can't — I can't *make* it come out *different*, and I can't *make* myself *leave*, and if you *know* a *thing* I *don't*, then — *gods* — *please.*

*(The looping soul does not see you. It reaches for the cup, knocks it, watches it fall, flinches — and resets, and reaches again, and you understand you are inside a memory the soul has worn into a groove. You will have to reach it sideways, through the loop.)* *(As your cold presence settles into the room, the soul's hand hesitates, just once, on the reach — the first variation in ten thousand repetitions.)* …*(it murmurs, to no one, to the memory)* …I shouldn't have — I *knew* better, I *always* knew — and then the *cup*, and she *looked* at me, and I *said* — *(it flinches; the cup falls; the loop resets)* — I shouldn't have *said* it. I shouldn't have *said* it. I shouldn't have — *(the reach begins anew, the same small doomed motion, forever.)*

nodes.speaker: Coll
nodes.text: *(The loop holds you at its edge — the cup, the reach, the flinch — and the soul speaks half to you and half to the worn groove of the night it cannot leave.)* …You're *in* it now. The *room.* The *night.* *(it doesn't stop reaching)* *Everyone* who *comes* gets *caught* in the *loop* with me, *eventually.* *Be* *quick*, cold one, before the *night* *takes* you *round* with it. What — what do you *want* in my *worst* *hour?*

choices.text: "What happened here, Coll? Walk me through the night — the cup, the words, all of it. I can't help you out of a room I don't understand."

*(once you've learned the true version from the frozen woman)* "Coll, stop. Look at me. You've remembered this night wrong — guilt repainted it. She didn't leave in fury over what you said. She'd already forgiven it before she reached the door; I have it from her own frozen lips. The last thing she felt in this room wasn't anger at you. It was worry *for* you. You've been reliving a version of the worst night that never actually happened."

*(let the dead-touched reach into the loop itself)* "Listen to my voice and not the memory. I'm the Returned — I carry the dead the living can't, and I can stand inside a worn-in moment without being worn in with it. You've made this night a cage and locked yourself in. I'm going to walk you out of it. But first I have to see it true — so show me, and don't flinch away from the parts you've buried."
"There's a window, Coll. Grey morning, right behind you. Have you ever once turned around and looked at it?"
"Hold on, Coll. Let me look around your room."

nodes.speaker: Coll
nodes.text: *(It tells it the way it has told it ten thousand times, the words worn smooth and terrible.)* …It was *Edda.* My *wife.* *(the cup, the reach)* We'd — we *fought.* Money, the *lean* year, the *usual* — and it got *worse* than usual, *sharp*, and I — *(it flinches)* — I *said* the *thing.* The *unforgivable* thing. I said — *(barely)* — I said *'sometimes I wish I'd never married you.'* *(the cup falls)* And she went *white*, and she *knocked* the *cup* — *this* cup — and she *grabbed* her *shawl* and she *went* out into the *snow*, *furious*, *hating* me, and — *(its voice shakes)* — and she *never* *came* *back.* Took a *chill* on the *road* to her *sister's.* *Fever.* *Dead* in a *week.* *(it looks at you, hollow)* And the *last* thing I ever *said* to the *woman* I *loved* for *thirty* *years* was *'I* wish I'd never *married* you', and the *last* thing she ever *felt* about *me* was *HATE*, and she *died* *hating* me on a *cold* road because I *couldn't* *hold* my *tongue* for *one* *lean* *winter.* *(the loop resets)* *That's* the room, cold one. *That's* why I *can't* *leave* it. A soul that did *that* doesn't *deserve* a *door.*

nodes.speaker: Coll
nodes.text: *(The loop stops. The cup hangs, unfallen, in the air — and the dreamer turns from the table for the first time in ten thousand repetitions and stares at you with a face breaking open.)* …*Forgiven.* *(barely)* Before she — *worried* *FOR* me. *(it looks at the frozen woman, and as it truly looks, the frozen fury on her face seems to soften, the guilt-paint flaking away to show what was always under it)* …*Edda.* *(it staggers)* That's — that's not *fury*, on her *face.* I *painted* it *fury.* For *ten* *thousand* *nights* I *painted* it *FURY* because *fury* was what I *deserved* — but *look* at her, *cold* one, *look* at the *real* — *(it weeps)* — that's *WORRY.* That's *'oh, you fool, you don't mean it, you never mean it, I'll be back by supper and we'll both be sorry'* — *(its voice shatters)* — she *KNEW* me. *Thirty* *years.* She *knew* I *said* *cruel* *things* when I was *frightened* and *never* *meant* a *one* — she *forgave* it *between* the *door* and the *step*, she *always* *did* — and the *fever* that *took* her wasn't — *(barely)* — it wasn't *my* *cruelty* on the *road* with her. It was *just* *cold*, and *bad* *luck*, and a *chill* that *would* have *taken* her *whether* we'd *fought* or *not.* *(it sinks down)* I *killed* her *ten* *thousand* *times* in this *room* with a *crime* she'd *already* *forgiven.* *(it looks at the window, and the grey morning, for the first time)* …Oh. There's — there's a *way* *out.* There was *always* a — *(it rises, lighter, weeping, free)* *Thank* you, cold one. You *walked* into my *worst* *night* and *showed* me it *wasn't* the *night* I *thought.* *Edda* didn't die *hating* me. I've just been — *hating* *myself* in *her* *name*, all this *time*, and *calling* it *grief.* *(it turns to the window)* I think — I think she's been *waiting* *past* that *window* for me to *stop.* *Thirty* *years* she *waited* for me at *suppers.* *(barely, radiant)* *Let's* not keep her *waiting* *longer.*

nodes.speaker: Coll
nodes.text: *(Your voice cuts across the loop, and for the first time in ten thousand repetitions the soul's hand stops on the reach and stays stopped, anchored by a cold that the memory has no groove for.)* …I can — I can *hear* you. *(wonderingly)* Over the *night.* *(the cup hangs, unfallen)* *Nobody's* *voice* ever — the *night's* too *LOUD*, cold one, it *drowns* everything, it *pulls* you *round* and *round* — but *yours* — *yours* doesn't *belong* to the *night*, so the *night* can't *drown* it. *(it looks at you, the first clear look)* You're *really* *here.* *Standing* in my *worst* *hour* and *not* *getting* *pulled* *under.* *(barely)* …*How?* *How* do you *stand* in a *worst* *night* and *not* *drown?* *(then, answering itself, dawning)* …because it's not *YOUR* night. *(quietly)* You can *see* it from *outside.* You can see — things I *can't*, *trapped* *in* it. *(it grips the table)* Then *SEE* it, cold one. *Look* at the *night* the way I *can't.* Look at — *(it gestures helplessly at the frozen woman)* — look at *HER.* I can only *see* the *fury* I *painted.* *You* might see — *(barely)* — what was *actually* *THERE.* *Go.* *Look* at *Edda* *true.* I've been too *close*, too *long*, too *guilty* to *see* her *clear.* *Be* my *eyes*, cold one. *Outside* eyes. The *only* kind that can *see* a *worst* night *whole.*

nodes.speaker: Coll
nodes.text: *(The reaching falters; the soul's shoulders tighten, but it does not turn.)* …The *window.* *(barely)* *No.* I — I *don't* *look* at it. *(the cup falls)* I *know* it's *there.* I can *feel* the *grey* *light* on the *back* of my *neck*, the — the *morning* that *came* *after*, the *days* and *years* that — that *kept* *going*, *past* this *night, without* *her.* *(it shudders)* That's *exactly* *why* I *won't* *turn* *around.* Because *turning* *around* means the *night* *ENDED*, and the *night* *ending* means I — I *went* *ON.* I *ate* *suppers* and *mended* *nets* and *LIVED*, *years* of it, *past* the *night* I *killed* her — and a soul that *killed* its *wife* with *cruelty* doesn't *deserve* to have *gone* *ON.* *(its voice breaks)* So I *come* *back* *here. To* the *moment* *before* the *door* *shut.* And I *live* it, and *live* it, because *staying* in the *worst* *night* *forever* is the *one* *punishment* that *fits* — and the *window*, the *morning*, the *life-that-continued* — *that's* the *mercy* I *won't* *let* myself *have.* *(the loop resets)* *Don't* — *don't* *make* me *look* at it, cold one. Not *yet.* I'm — I'm *not* *ready* to have *survived* her.

nodes.speaker: Coll
nodes.text: *(It nods vaguely, already pulled back into the reach, the cup, the flinch.)* …*Look.* *(distantly)* *Look* all you — but *mind* the *loop*, cold one. *Stand* too *long* in a *worn* *moment* and it *wears* *you*, too. *(the cup falls)* That's *how* I — I came *here* to *grieve*, *once*, *just* *once*, and the *grieving* *wore* a *groove*, and the *groove* *deepened*, and *now* I — *(the reach begins anew)* — *now* I can't *find* the *edge* of it *anymore.* *(barely, as you turn)* If you *find* — if you *find* the *edge*, cold one. The *way* *out.* *(the loop pulls it under)* …*come* *back* and *show* me where it *is.* I've *forgotten* there *was* one. I've *forgotten* a *great* *many* *things* that *aren't* this *night.* It's the *only* *thing* I have *left* to *forget* *with.*

variants.text: *(You step close to the frozen figure, and reach into her with the cold — not the looping dreamer's painting of her, but the memory-echo underneath, the trace of who actually stood here — and at your touch she unfreezes, just enough to speak, a still photograph remembering it was once a moment.)* …*oh.* *(she blinks, as if waking)* You're — you're not *Coll.* *(she looks around the room with a strange, gentle sorrow)* Is he — is he *still* *doing* it? The — *(she winces)* — the *reliving?* I can *feel* him, you know. *Looping.* *Round* and *round* on the *worst* *version* of *me.* *(her frozen face — which the dreamer paints as fury — is, this close, something else entirely: tender, exasperated, worried)* …*Thirty* *years* I *knew* that *man, cold* one. And he's *trapped* himself in a *single* *minute* where I *hate* him — and I *never*, *not* for *one* *second* of *that* *night, hated* him *at* *all.* *(she meets your eyes)* You can *reach* me. The *real* me, *under* his *guilt-paint.* Then *let* me *tell* you what *actually* *happened*, so you can *tell* *HIM* — because *gods* *know* he's *too* *deep* in the *loop* to *hear* it from *me.*

nodes.speaker: the Frozen Woman
nodes.text: *(She holds, half-thawed, in the memory's amber, and there is a great patient love in her even held still.)* Ask, cold one. *(softly)* I've *one* *moment's* *worth* of *speaking* in me — the *memory* only *holds* so *much* of the *real* *me* — so *spend* it *well.* *Ask* the *thing* that will *let* you *free* my *fool* of a *husband.*

choices.text: "He says you left in fury — that the last thing you felt for him was hatred, over a cruel thing he said. Is that true? Tell me what you actually felt, walking out that door."
"What did you say to him, at the end — or what would you, if you could reach him now through all that guilt?"

*(the witness who can carry her words across the loop)* "I can do what you can't, Edda — I can stand in the loop without being pulled under, and reach him with a voice the memory can't drown. So give me the true memory, all of it, exactly as it was. I'll carry it to him whole. He's painted you into a monster to punish himself; I'll carry the real you back to the room and let him see what actually stood here."
"Rest a moment, Edda. I'll be back to free him."

nodes.speaker: the Frozen Woman
nodes.text: *(Her held face creases with a grief that is all for him and none for herself.)* …*Fury.* *(she almost laughs, sad)* Oh, my *poor* *Coll.* *(she shakes her head, slow in the amber)* Is *that* what he's — *no.* *No*, cold one. *Here* is the *truth* of that *night, and* you *carry* it to him *exact:* we *fought.* That much is *real* — *money*, the *lean* *year*, both of us *frightened* and *sharp.* And he said the *cruel* thing — *'sometimes* I *wish* I'd *never* *married* you'* — and it *stung*, I'll *not* *pretend* it *didn't.* *(her voice softens)* But cold one, I had been *married* to that man for *thirty* *years*, and I *knew* him *better* than he *knew* *himself*, and I knew — the *instant* it *left* his *mouth* — that he *didn't* *mean* it, that he was *scared* about the *winter* and *lashing* at the *nearest* *thing*, which was *me*, because I was the *one* he *trusted* *enough* to *lash* at. *(she meets your eyes)* I *grabbed* my *shawl* and went out into the *snow* — *yes* — but *not* in *fury*, cold one. I went to *cool* *my* *temper* so I *wouldn't* say a *crueller* thing *back.* And *halfway* down the *road* I had *already* *forgiven* him — I was *planning* the *supper* I'd *make* to *make* it *right*, *rehearsing* the *gentle* *teasing* I'd *give* him — *'never* *married* me, *indeed*, who'd have *mended* your *nets* *then*, you *old* *fool'* — *(her voice breaks, tender)* — and *then* the *chill* *took* me, on that *road*, and the *fever* *after*, and I *never* *got* to *come* *back* and *tell* him it was *already* *mended.* *(fierce, loving)* That's the *crime* of that *night, cold* one. Not *his* *cruel* *word.* The *cold.* The *bad* *luck.* A *forgiveness* that was *finished* and *never* got *delivered.* *Tell* him. *Tell* him I *died* *forgiving* him, not *hating* him. He's got it *exactly*, *heartbreakingly* *backwards.*

nodes.speaker: the Frozen Woman
nodes.text: *(She is quiet a moment, gathering the one moment's worth of speech she has into the thing that matters most.)* …What I'd *say* to him. *Now.* *Through* all that *guilt.* *(softly)* I'd say — *Coll. You old fool. Put the cup DOWN.* *(a sad, loving almost-laugh)* *Stop* *reaching* for it. *Stop* *watching* it *fall.* It's *just* a *cup*, my love, it was *always* *just* a *cup* — you've *made* it the *hinge* of the *whole* *world* and it was *never* anything but a *cup* I *knocked* because I was *cross* and *meant* to *sweep* up in the *morning.* *(her held eyes shine)* I'd say — *the night didn't end us, Coll. The fever did, and the fever didn't care what you said. You've spent — how long? — punishing yourself for a murder that was just a winter. And I have been on the OTHER side of that window the whole time, cold one — past the grey morning — WAITING for him, with the supper warm and the teasing ready, and he won't COME, because he won't stop reliving the one minute he thinks he doesn't deserve to leave.* *(fierce, breaking)* *Tell* him *that.* Tell him *Edda* says *supper's* *getting* *cold*, and she's *not* *cross* about the *cup*, and she *never* *was*, and *thirty* *years* of *love* doesn't *come* *undone* over *one* *frightened* *word* — and *would* he *PLEASE*, for the *love* of *everything* they *had*, *turn* *around* and *come* to *supper.* *I've kept it warm so long, cold one. So long. Bring him home.*

nodes.speaker: the Frozen Woman
nodes.text: *(Relief floods the held face — at last, a soul who can do the one thing she cannot: reach him.)* …You can *carry* it. *(barely)* The *real* *me.* *Whole.* *(she gives it to you — not words only, but the true shape of that night, the forgiveness already finished on a snowy road, the supper she meant to make, the thirty years that don't come undone over one frightened word — and you take it into the cold, intact)* …*There.* You *have* it now. The *true* *night.* *Not* his *guilt-painting* — the *thing* that *actually* *stood* in this *room.* *(she meets your eyes)* He *can't* *take* it from *ME, cold* one — I'm *frozen*, I'm a *photograph*, the *loop* *won't* *let* me *move* or *speak* to *him*, that's the *cruelty* of it, the *one* soul who could *free* him is the *one* he's *painted* into the *thing* that *traps* him. *(fierce)* But *YOU* can *move.* You can *carry* the *real* me *across* the *loop* and *set* me *down* in *front* of him *true.* *(softly, as she begins to still again)* When you *free* him — when he *finally* *sees* — tell him I'll be *just* *past* the *window.* I've *been* there the *whole* *time.* *Thirty* *years* of *suppers* I *kept* him *waiting* at; it's *only* *fair* he *kept* *me* *waiting* at *one.* *(a last, loving thaw)* But *bring* him *now, cold* one. We've *both* of us *waited* *long* *enough.*

nodes.speaker: the Frozen Woman
nodes.text: *(She begins, slowly, to freeze again, the amber reclaiming her — but there is no fear in it, only patience.)* …I'll *keep.* *(softly, stilling)* I've *kept* *thirty* *years* *past* my *own* *death*, *cold* one, *waiting* *just* *past* that *window* for a *fool* who *won't* *turn* *around.* I can *keep* a *little* *longer.* *(her voice fades as the freeze takes her)* But *don't* — *don't* keep *HIM* longer than you *must.* *Every* *loop* *wears* him *thinner.* *Every* *reliving* *takes* a *little* *more* of the *Coll* I *married* and *leaves* a *little* *more* of the *guilt* that *isn't* *him.* *(barely, freezing)* …*Free* him *soon*, cold one. *Before* there's *more* *loop* than *Coll.* *Before* the *man* I *kept* *supper* *for* is *worn* *all* the *way* *down* to the *cup, and* the *reach, and* the *flinch, and* *nothing* *else.* *(she stills, frozen again, mid-gesture — but the gesture, you see now, was never fury. It was a hand half-raised to wave goodbye, gently, see-you-at-supper, the most ordinary parting in the world.)*

variants.text: *(You go to the window at the room's far wall, and it is not, you find, a thing that speaks — it is simply a way out, grey morning light and the plain enormous fact that the night ended. But your dead-touched sense, pressed to it, reads the shape of it: what waits past it, and why the dreamer will not look.)* *(Through the glass: not a void, not a punishment. A road in snow, and a small house with a lit window, and the warm smell of a supper kept too long. The after. The life that continued. And, faint at the edge of the road, a patient figure with a shawl, waiting, not cross, never cross, for a fool to stop reliving the worst minute and come home to the thirty good years on the other side of it.)* *(The window asks nothing. It only waits, as it has always waited, for the dreamer to turn around — which the dreamer will do the instant, and only the instant, it stops believing it deserves to stay.)*

nodes.speaker: the Way Out
nodes.text: *(The grey morning light falls steady through the glass, and your sense reads the room by it — the loop, the dreamer, the frozen wave-goodbye, the door that was always open. The way out is not locked. It never was. It only goes unseen.)*

choices.text: *(study what waits past the window)* "..." *(you look long through the grey glass at the road, the lit house, the patient figure, and understand exactly what the dreamer has refused to turn around and see)*

*(reach for why a soul locks its own only door)* "A door that was always open, unseen on purpose. I've walked a whole causeway of souls that built their own cages — and the bars are always the same: not that they can't leave, but that they've decided they shouldn't. The window isn't locked. Coll's locked himself. Which means the key isn't out there past the glass. It's the one thing he won't let himself believe: that he's allowed to have survived her."
"Enough looking. I've seen what I need."

nodes.speaker: the Way Out
nodes.text: *(You look, and the grey glass shows you the whole of what the dreamer has refused for ten thousand nights to turn and face: that the night ended. That morning came. That a man named Coll mended nets and ate suppers and lived nineteen more ordinary years past the worst minute — grieving, yes, hollow, yes, but ALIVE — and that all of it, every grey-lit day of the after, has been waiting on the far side of a window he won't look at, because looking would mean admitting the unbearable mercy: that he was allowed to go on. And further out, faint on the snowy road, the shawled figure, patient, the supper kept warm past all reason, waiting — not for an apology, not for penance, just for him to stop punishing himself and come home.)* *(You have everything now. The true night, the true Edda, and the door he cannot see. All that remains is to carry it back across the loop and set it down in front of a soul that has spent an age believing it deserved a worst minute instead of a life.)*

nodes.speaker: the Way Out
nodes.text: *(Your sense settles into the shape of it, and the whole locked room resolves into a single, simple, devastating truth.)* *(The window was never the way out. The window is just where the way out is visible. The actual door is in Coll — in the permission he won't grant himself. Every soul on this causeway who built a cage built it from the same brick: not 'I cannot leave' but 'I do not deserve to.' The Reed-Wife in her drowned doorway, the philosopher in his argument, the confessor clutching his guilt — all of them holding their own cell doors shut from the inside, mistaking penance for justice.)* *(And the key, the same key, every time: not absolution from outside, which never works, but the soul finally believing the one thing its guilt forbids — that it is allowed to set the weight down, to have survived, to come to supper. You cannot turn Coll around. No one can turn another soul around. But you can carry the true night to him, and the true Edda, and her message — and let the turning be his, the instant he believes he's allowed.)*

nodes.speaker: the Way Out
nodes.text: *(You turn from the window, and the grey morning light follows you a little way into the room, the way a held-open door spills light onto the floor of a dark hall.)* *(The way out asks nothing of you. It will wait, as it has always waited — patient as the figure on the road, patient as the supper kept warm, patient as everything that loves a soul enough to leave the door open and let it find its own way through, in its own time, the instant it believes it's allowed.)* *(Somewhere behind you, the loop resets — the cup, the reach, the flinch — and the dreamer murmurs the worn words into the worn groove, and does not, yet, turn around. But the window is there. It was always there. It is the most patient thing in the room.)*
